figures.caption: Fig. 3: Comparing planning time and cost for CT vs. DT planners, with U = [−1, 1]2. The top and bottom plots depict results of the RRT- and SST planner, respectively.

TABLE I: Benchmark planner performance results for the CT and DT planners; using RRT and SST. Each entry is the mean of 100 simulations. The best (lowest) time and cost between CT and DT variants are bolded.
Fig. 2: Continuous-time Gaussian belief space planner trajectories across six environments, where X ∈ [0, 50]2.

Fig. 1: A pitfall in discrete-time, belief-space planning is to evaluate safety only at discrete time, when the measurements are received (i.e., at bt and b+t+1). This is insufficient because the state (and its belief) naturally grows in continuous-time between discretetime belief updates due to process and measurement uncertainty, potentially intersecting with obstacles.
